sections.paragraph:
ἀντίλυτρον, ου, τό (Polyaenus, Exc. 52, 7 Melber; Orphica: Lithica 593 Abel πάντων ἀ; schol. on Nicander, Alexiph. 560 ἀντ. … ὑπὲρ τῶν βοῶν; Origen, Field, Hexapla II 170 Field on Ps 48:9; Hesych.; Cosmas and Damian 40, 30; a late pap PLond IV, 1343, 31 [VIII a.d.]) ransom ἀ. ὑπὲρ πάντων 1 Ti 2:6 (s. Mlt. 105; Jos., Ant. 14, 107 λύτρον ἀντὶ πάντων=ransom for the return of all).—TW. Spicq. DELG s.v. λύω.

Abel Abel = FA., Grammaire du Grec biblique—List 6

schol. schol. = scholion, scholia, scholiast

Nicander Nicander , III/II b.c.—List 5

Field, Hexapla Hexapla , s. Orig(enes) II–III a.d.—List 5

Field Field = FF.—List 6

Hesych Hesych , V a.d.—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

Mlt. Mlt. = JMoulton; with page number alone specified, this abbr. refers to A Grammar of NT Greek I: Prolegomena—List 6

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 89
